items.title: How beer and AI are changing the course of Canberra's economic future

children.content: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

items.title: 'Not what the community wanted': Big Splash lease spared despite backlash

children.content: ACT planning authorities have decided they will not follow through with a threat to terminate the lease of Big Splash water park, saying they are now satisfied the operators have made moves to reopen later this year.

image.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

items.title: Want to buy a football team? Why this club's next chapter is unwritten

children.content: Canberra United is once again without an owner and facing an uncertain future, in a tale all too familiar for players and fans of the A-League's only stand-alone women's team.

items.title: 'Carry the hope': Thousands gather for Anzac Day service in Canberra

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs are among those to gather in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

items.title: Canberrans with disability fear NDIS changes will cut vital support

image.altText: A woman sitting on a couch looking at the camera.

items.title: Payroll error leaves thousands of hospital staff waiting for fortnightly wages

children.content: Thousands of North Canberra Hospital employees were not paid on time this fortnight, with some staff raising concerns on social media that they had received pay slips but no corresponding bank deposits, after a payroll processing issue.

image.altText: North Canberra Hospital exterior

items.title: 'Self-belief, respect': Indigenous boxers building strength with David Pocock

children.content: A Canberra Indigenous-led boxing camp is helping young fighters hone their skills for life inside and outside the ring in its first development camp, continuing a proud tradition of First Nations involvement in the sport.

items.title: 'Security was extraordinarily lax': ABC's Americas editor on how the shooting played out

children.content: ABC's Americas editor John Lyons said there was less security than your average Australian airport at the event.
He describes people screaming around him as the shots were heard.
Cabinet ministers and other important people were being plucked from tables as the Secret Service started running through the room in "extraordinary" scenes.

children.content: Peter Drew has been hanging his controversial posters around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

items.title: Sabastian Sawe ran the marathon in an 'unbelievable time'. Here's why we can believe in it

children.content: Sabastian Sawe has smashed through one of athletics' unbreakable barriers by running a marathon faster than two hours. Some call it unbelievable, but the Kenyan runner has done everything he can to ensure it stands the test of time.

items.title: Paralympic champion shares emotional volatility after traumatic brain injury

children.content: Alexa Leary sustained a traumatic brain injury (TBI) after a cycling accident at age 19. The two-time Paralympic gold medallist turned author is calling for a day to recognise TBI and the unique challenges it brings.
